goal_pattern: |⟪x - y, z⟫_ℝ| ≤ ‖x - y‖ * ‖z‖ (Cauchy-Schwarz on real inner product)
tactic: intro t; have h := @norm_inner_le_norm ℝ E _ _ _ _ _; simpa [Real.norm_eq_abs] using h
note: explicit @ + scalar field needed; abs_inner_le_norm does not exist in this Mathlib

goal_pattern: ⟪a, c⟫_ℝ = ⟪b, c⟫_ℝ + ⟪a - b, c⟫_ℝ (inner-product bilinearity split)
‖∇f(x + t•(y-x)) - ∇f(x)‖ ≤ L*t*‖y-x‖ on t ∈ [0,1] given LipschitzWith L (gradient f)
tactic: hL.dist_le_mul + simp [dist_eq_norm] + γt - x = t•(y-x) + norm_smul + abs_of_nonneg + calc chain

goal_pattern: shbIter β η f' x₀ xPrev (t + 1) - shbIter ... t = β • (... - ...) - η • f' ...
context: Use binary subtraction form `β•(d) - η•(g)` rather than `-η • g + β•(d)` to avoid abel failing on mixed Z/ℝ scalar normalization (right-assoc `•` makes `-η • g` parse as `(-1)•(η•g)` and abel cannot equate that with `((-1)•η)•g` from neg_smul).

goal_pattern: f (shbIter β η f' x₀ xPrev (t+1)) ≤ ... + ⟨f'(...), x_{t+1}-x_t⟩ + (l/2)‖x_{t+1}-x_t‖²
context: Single-step SHB descent reduces directly to the gradient-form descent lemma; no work on top of the existing Generated lemma

goal_pattern: IsClosed {p : ℝ × ℝ | p.2 ≤ 2 * (1 + p.1) / L}
tactic: refine isClosed_le ?_ ?_ ; exact continuous_snd ; exact ((continuous_const.add continuous_fst).const_mul 2).div_const L
context: Stability inequality is a continuous-≤ relation; explicit composition of continuity lemmas works without invoking the `continuity` macro

name: layer3-multi-file-decomposition
trigger: Layer-3 problem (≥150 LOC, telescoping over Finset.range, registry-backed helpers available)
action: Import sub-helpers as named modules (LeanAgent.Generated.foo); reuse via `foo` not in-line. Avoids single-file budget pressure.
evidence: #11 gradient_method (167 LOC, imports #06+#07), #12 proximal_gradient_method_converge (258 LOC, imports #03+#06+#08); both CERTIFIED.

name: pnat-to-nat-destructure
trigger: Theorem statement quantifies over k : ℕ+ but proof body works with k = n + 1 for n : ℕ
action: Use `rintro ⟨n, hn_pos⟩` then `rcases Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hn_pos.ne' with ⟨k, rfl⟩`; finish with `show ... ≤ ... 1 / (2 * ((k+1 : ℕ) : ℝ) * t) * ...` then `rw [hcast]` where hcast bridges the ℕ+→ℕ→ℝ coercion.
evidence: #12 final calc step; cleaner than `match k.val, k.pos with | k+1, _ =>` which leaves push_cast unable to fold the double coercion.

name: aux-sequence-synthesis
trigger: Lyapunov-style proof: NL input mentions 'Lyapunov function', 'extrapolated point', 'auxiliary sequence', or the proof requires inventing a non-iterate sequence
action: Define `private noncomputable def auxSeq (alg : ...) : N -> E` at the top level (not inline). Prove 1-2 'bridge lemmas' relating auxSeq to the algorithm's iterates. Stage-1 Decomposer should treat auxSeq as a first-class atom alongside the lemmas.
evidence: #13 Nesterov_first_converge: zSeq def + xkk1_eq_combine + y_eq_combine; closed in 304 LOC with 5 tactical fix rounds.

name: match-scalars-for-vector-identities
trigger: Goal is a vector equation `c1 . v1 + c2 . v2 + ... = c1' . v1 + c2' . v2 + ...` where coefficients involve real division (e.g. 1/gamma_k); abel cannot close because the coefficients are not pure additive
action: Use `match_scalars <;> field_simp <;> ring`. The match_scalars tactic separates each basis vector's coefficient as a separate goal; field_simp + ring then closes each scalar goal.
evidence: #13 y_eq_combine succ case: y (n+1) = (1 - gamma (n+1)) . x (n+1) + gamma (n+1) . zSeq (n+1) reduced to 2 scalar identities, each closed by `field_simp <;> ring`.
